name: Ultraviolet (UV) Radiation
definition: Ultraviolet radiation exposure (from sunlight or artificial sources) can influence skin and immune biology. In autoimmune research, UV exposure is discussed as a potential trigger for disease flares in some conditions (context-dependent). GENARCH presents this as educational, population-level context without clinical recommendations.

systems_affected.mechanism_summary: UV can induce skin injury responses and modulate immune signaling; effects are context- and dose-dependent.

sensitive_windows.mechanism_rationale: UV exposure is episodic and seasonal; autoimmune literature often discusses contextual sensitivity rather than a single developmental window.

references.title: Ultraviolet radiation and autoimmune disease mechanisms: overview (review)
